classification.label: Miscellaneous
classification.rationale_short: No clear indication of confidentiality or other specific clause type

analysis.plain_english_summary: This is a placeholder summary for clause c002.

why: This clause is a placeholder and does not introduce significant business risk.

reasons: Insufficient context to accurately assess the clause's implications.

clause.text:
NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

 

 

This Non-Disclosure Agreement (the Agreement)
is made and entered into effective December 18, 2017 by and between 
Pareteum Corporation
, a Delaware corporation having
its corporate address at:  100 Park Avenue, Suite 1600, New York City, New York 10017, USA (TEUM), and 
iPass
Inc.
, a Delaware corporation having its address at: 3800 Bridge Parkway, Redwood Shores, California 94065, USA (iPass),
either both of which may be hereinafter referred to as "the Party" or "the Parties."

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: keyword baseline

analysis.plain_english_summary: This is a non-disclosure agreement between Pareteum Corporation and iPass Inc.

why: The agreement is between two companies, and the scope of confidentiality is not specified.

reasons: The scope of confidentiality is not specified, and the parties may have different expectations.

clause.text:
WHEREAS

 

The Parties desire to discuss certain business
transactions and to exchange information for the purpose of exploring a potential business relationship for the benefit of the
Parties and/or to sign a business contract that shall include confidential technical or business information of each Party or entitle
each Party to exchange information for the execution of this business contract. In order to facilitate these discussions and in
order for the Parties to receive from each other, either orally or in writing, certain technical and business information under
terms that will protect the confidential and proprietary nature of such information, the Parties have entered into this Agreement.

  

NOW THEREFORE, THE PARTIES AGREE AS
FOLLOWS;

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: The clause discusses the protection of confidential and proprietary information.

analysis.plain_english_summary: The parties agree to keep confidential information shared during discussions and negotiations.

why: The clause is a standard confidentiality agreement, which is a common practice in business negotiations.

reasons: The clause is a standard confidentiality agreement, but the specific terms and conditions may vary depending on the parties' intentions and the context of the agreement.

clause.text:
1.
The Definition of "Confidential Information": 
The term "Confidential Information"
shall mean all information disclosed by one Party to the other Party, whether orally, in written, electronic or other format, and
whether disclosed by a Partys agents, principals, employees or representatives, and whether to the other Party's agent's
principals, employees or representatives. Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all ideas improvements,
inventions, methodologies, works and other innovations of any kind, authored, conceived, developed, made or reduced to practice
by the disclosing Party, whether or not eligible for copyright, patent, trademark, trade secret or other legal protection (including,
without limitation, formulas, processes, databases, mechanical and electronic hardware, electronic components, computers and their
parts, computer programs and their documentation, encoding techniques, marketing and new product plans, production, processes,
advertising, packaging and marketing techniques, marketing plans, product plans, technical plans, business strategies, strategic
alliances and partners, financial information, engineering data, methodologies and processes, forecasts, personnel information,
customer and prospective customer lists, trade secrets, product design, capabilities, specifications, the identify of potential
and actual customers, and suppliers and all documentation, materials and media provided by one Party to the other).

classification.label: Definition of Confidential Info
classification.rationale_short: Clause defines Confidential Information

flags.name: unlimited_liability_language
flags.evidence_snippet:
incipals, employees or representatives. Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all ideas improvements,
inventions, methodologies, works and other innovations
flags.explanation: Language suggests liability may be uncapped or broadly expanded.

analysis.plain_english_summary: Confidential Information includes all information shared between parties, regardless of format, including ideas, inventions, and innovations.

why: The language may suggest unlimited liability, which could lead to significant financial exposure.

comparison: Typical confidentiality clauses may limit liability or specify exceptions
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a liability cap or specifying exceptions to limit exposure.

reasons: The language is broad and may be open to interpretation, and the potential for unlimited liability is a significant concern.

citations.evidence:
incipals, employees or representatives. Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all ideas improvements,
inventions, methodologies, works and other innovations

clause.text:
2.
Protection of "Confidential Information": 
In consideration of each Party's disclosure
of Confidential Information to the other Party, each Party agrees with respect to the Confidential Information received from the
other Party, that it:

 

(a)
shall maintain such Confidential Information in the strictest confidence;

 

Pareteum Corporation | 100 Park Ave. | Suite 1600 | New York | NY 10017 | United States
Tel. +1 (212) 984.1096
www.Pareteum.com
NYSE MKT: TEUM

 

 

(b)
shall not disclose, transfer or otherwise make available any of such Confidential Information to
any third party, unless such Confidential Information must be disclosed for the purposes contemplated herein, or under legal compulsion
to disclose any such Confidential Information, in which event each Party shall, prior to such disclosure, obtain written consent
from the other Party and obtain from the third person a written agreement acknowledging the binding effect of these restrictions
regarding the Confidential Information; and

 

(c)
shall not directly, indirectly or in concert with any person, use the Confidential Information
for any purpose other than evaluating the prospective business relationship with the other Party in accordance with the introduction
.

 

Each Party shall take reasonable
measures to protect the Confidential Information of the other Party. Those measures shall not be less than the measures taken to
protect the receiving Partys own confidential information. Confidential Information of the other Party may be provided to
a Partys employees only on a need-to-know basis, and prior to such provision, the Party will notify each employee to whom
such disclosure is made that such Confidential Information is received in confidence and shall be kept in confidence by such employee.

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: The clause is about protecting confidential information.

analysis.plain_english_summary: Parties agree to keep confidential information confidential and not disclose it to third parties.

why: The risk of unauthorized disclosure is low, as the parties are required to keep the information confidential and only disclose it under certain circumstances.

comparison: The parties' obligations to keep confidential information confidential are more stringent than usual.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a provision for the destruction of confidential information after it is no longer needed.

reasons: The parties' obligations to keep confidential information confidential are not explicitly defined, and the scope of the confidential information is not clearly limited.

clause.text:
3.
Excluded Information: 
This Agreement shall not apply to any information:

 

(a)
that has been or which becomes publicly known, through no wrongful act of either Party;

 

(b)
which is required to be disclosed in order to comply with applicable law or regulation or with
any requirement imposed by judicial or administrative process or any governmental or court order.

classification.label: Exceptions
classification.rationale_short: Defines what information is excluded from the agreement.

analysis.plain_english_summary: Exceptions to the agreement that excludes information that is publicly known or required to be disclosed by law or regulation.

why: This clause is intended to clarify the scope of the agreement and avoid disputes over what information is excluded.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding specific examples of information that would be excluded, such as trade secrets or confidential business information.

reasons: The clause is clear and well-written, but the suggested revision could improve its effectiveness.

clause.text:
4.
No Licenses or Warranties: 
Each Partys Confidential Information and all rights thereto
shall remain such Partys sole property. Each Party recognizes that the disclosure of Confidential Information by the disclosing
Party shall not be construed as granting any rights, by license or otherwise, concerning any Confidential Information, except as
may be explicitly created by this Agreement. Each Party acknowledges that the other Partys Confidential Information includes
valuable trade secrets. Neither Party has any obligation to disclose Confidential Information to the other Party. NEITHER PARTY
MAKES ANY REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY AS TO THE ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, CONDITION, FITNESS and MERCHANTABILITY, OR PERFORMANCE OF
ITS CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: Clause defines confidentiality obligations and ownership

analysis.plain_english_summary: Each party retains ownership of their confidential information and does not grant any rights to the other party, except as explicitly stated in this agreement.

why: The parties are simply clarifying their respective ownership and rights to confidential information, which is a standard practice in agreements.

comparison: industry standard is to grant a license or warranty for confidential information
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a license or warranty for confidential information to ensure clarity and consistency with industry standards.

reasons: The clause may be too broad and may not cover all potential scenarios, but it is a common practice in agreements to retain ownership of confidential information.

clause.text:
5.
Remedies: 
If either Party causes a disclosure of the other Partys Confidential Information
in breach of the terms of this Agreement, the disclosing Party shall immediately report in writing the disclosure to the other
Party and shall save, defend, indemnify and hold the non-disclosing Party harmless from and against any and all liability and damages
suffered by the non-disclosing Party arising therefrom. In addition to the foregoing and without limitation thereof, the disclosing
Party shall cooperate in prosecuting any claims against third parties for unauthorized use of any Confidential Information. Each
Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure, use or disposition, whether actual or threatened, of any Confidential Information
shall cause irreparable harm, loss of business and significant injury to the disclosing Party, the scope of which would be difficult
to ascertain. Each Party agrees, therefore, that the disclosing Party has the right to obtain an immediate injunction against any
breach, threatened breach or attempted breach of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedies that may be available at law,
including without limitation, the recovery of expenses, costs and attorneys fees arising out of such breach, threatened
breach or attempted breach.

classification.label: Remedies / Injunctive Relief
classification.rationale_short: Clause discusses remedies for breach of confidentiality agreement

flags.name: unlimited_liability_language
flags.evidence_snippet:
by the non-disclosing Party arising therefrom. In addition to the foregoing and without limitation thereof, the disclosing
Party shall cooperate in prosecuting any claims against
flags.explanation: Language suggests liability may be uncapped or broadly expanded.

analysis.plain_english_summary: If a party breaches confidentiality, the other party can report the breach and seek damages, and the breaching party must help with claims against third parties.

why: The language suggests unlimited liability, which can be a significant risk for the non-disclosing party.

comparison: Typical indemnification clauses may not include unlimited liability language
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider revising to specify a cap on liability

reasons: The language is unclear about the scope of liability, and the use of 'without limitation' may expand liability beyond what is typical in similar agreements

citations.evidence:
by the non-disclosing Party arising therefrom. In addition to the foregoing and without limitation thereof, the disclosing
Party shall cooperate in prosecuting any claims against

clause.text:
6.
Return of "Confidential Information": 
All Confidential Information shall be returned
to the disclosing Parties promptly upon written request or, at the election of the disclosing Party, the Party that received the
Confidential Information shall certify said information has been destroyed and is no longer useable in any format.

 

Pareteum Corporation | 100 Park Ave. | Suite 1600 | New York | NY 10017 | United States
Tel. +1 (212) 984.1096
www.Pareteum.com
NYSE MKT: TEUM

classification.label: Return/Destruction
classification.rationale_short: Clause specifies return or destruction of confidential information.

analysis.plain_english_summary: The disclosing party must return or destroy confidential information upon request.

why: The return or destruction of confidential information is a standard practice in business agreements to protect sensitive information.

reasons: The clause is a standard provision in many business agreements, but the specific requirements for return or destruction may vary depending on the jurisdiction and industry.

clause.text:
7.
Securities:
 Parties hereby acknowledge, covenant and agree that they are aware that United
States securities laws may prohibit any person who has material, non-public information about a company (including a Party) from
purchasing or selling, directly or indirectly, securities of a company (including the Parties), or from communicating such information
to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such
securities.

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: Securities laws prohibit material non-public information disclosure

analysis.plain_english_summary: Parties agree not to use material, non-public information to buy or sell securities.

why: The clause only prohibits using material, non-public information for securities transactions, not other business activities.

comparison: Standard confidentiality clauses typically include broader prohibitions on using confidential information for personal gain.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a broader prohibition on using confidential information for personal gain.

reasons: The clause's scope is limited to securities transactions, which may not be the only way parties could use material, non-public information for personal gain.

clause.text:
8.
Prohibition of Disclosure: 
Neither Party hereto
shall in any way or in any form distribute, disclose, publicize, issue press releases, or advertise in any manner, including,
but not limited to, making representation in court pleadings, except as required by law, the discussions that gave rise to this
Agreement, the discussions or negotiations covered by this Agreement, this Agreement or the Confidential Information provided
pursuant to this Agreement, without first obtaining the prior written consent of the other Party.

classification.label: Permitted Disclosures
classification.rationale_short: Clause regulates disclosure of confidential information

analysis.plain_english_summary: Neither party can disclose confidential information without permission.

why: Disclosure of confidential information can cause significant reputational damage and financial loss.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding specific examples of permitted disclosures, such as in response to a court order.

reasons: The clause may not cover all possible scenarios, such as emergency situations where prompt disclosure is necessary.

clause.text:
9.
Term, Assignment and Survival: 
This Agreement shall be valid unless terminated by mutual
written Agreement. Each Partys obligations with respect to the Confidential Information, including but not limited to, sections
2,4,5,6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement and/or return of all Confidential Information from the latter date of either
termination or return of such information. Neither this Agreement nor any rights hereunder, in whole or in part, shall be assignable
or otherwise transferable by either Party.

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Survival of obligations after termination

analysis.plain_english_summary: The agreement can be terminated by mutual written agreement. Obligations related to confidential information survive termination. The agreement is not assignable or transferable.

why: The agreement can be terminated by mutual written agreement, which may reduce the risk of non-performance.

comparison: Standard term and survival clauses typically do not include the prohibition on assignment and transfer.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider revising to allow for assignment and transfer under certain conditions.

reasons: The clause is clear on its face, but the impact of the prohibition on assignment and transfer may be unclear in practice.

clause.text:
10.
Governing Law; Jurisdiction: 
This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance
with the laws of the State of New York, United States of America. In the event of any disputes arising under this Agreement, the
undersigned Parties without regard to any principles of conflicts of laws and waiving any defenses of forum non conveniens hereby
submit to the exclusive personal and subject matter jurisdiction of the State and Federal Courts situated in the Borough of Manhattan,
New York, New York.

classification.label: Governing Law
classification.rationale_short: Refers to the jurisdiction and laws governing the contract.

analysis.plain_english_summary: This contract will be governed by the laws of New York and any disputes will be resolved in the Manhattan courts.

why: The governing law and jurisdiction are clearly specified, reducing the risk of disputes and potential misinterpretation.

comparison: Most contracts specify a governing law and jurisdiction, but the specific choice of New York may impact the parties' rights and obligations.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider specifying a more neutral or industry-standard governing law to reduce potential risks.

reasons: The clause is clear and concise, but the choice of New York law may be subject to interpretation and potential challenges.

clause.text:
11.
Waiver: 
No failure by either Party to exercise any rights arising from default by the other
Party shall impair that right or constitute a waiver of it. No waiver by either Party of any covenant to be performed by the other
shall constitute a waiver of any later breach of covenant.

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Clause discusses waiver of rights and covenants, indicating term and survival of contract

analysis.plain_english_summary: Neither party's failure to enforce a right or waive a breach of a covenant will prevent them from enforcing that right or waiving that breach in the future.

why: This clause is intended to prevent one party from taking advantage of the other's leniency or delay in enforcing their rights.

comparison: This clause is consistent with standard contract language and does not deviate from industry norms.

reasons: The language of this clause is clear and unambiguous, but the application of this clause in a specific situation may depend on the context and the specific terms of the contract.

clause.text:
12.
Counterparts: 
This Agreement may be executed in two signed copies, each of which when taken
together shall be deemed but one original.

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Counterparts clause is part of agreement terms

analysis.plain_english_summary: The agreement can be signed in multiple copies, but all copies together are considered one original.

why: This clause does not introduce significant risk to the parties involved.

reasons: The clause is straightforward and does not introduce ambiguity.

clause.text:
13.
Severability: 
The validity or unenforceability of any provision or provisions of this Agreement
shall no affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision hereof, which shall remain in full force and effect.

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Refers to the survival of other provisions in case one is invalid or unenforceable.

analysis.plain_english_summary: If one part of the agreement is invalid or unenforceable, it won't affect the rest of the agreement.

why: This clause is a standard severability provision, which is common in contracts. It's unlikely to cause significant business risk.

comparison: Typically, severability provisions are more detailed, specifying which provisions are severable and which are not.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding more detail on which provisions are severable and which are not.

reasons: The clause is straightforward, but the specific circumstances of the agreement may affect its application.

clause.text:
14.
Entire Agreement; Amendment: 
This Agreement contains the entire understandings between and
among the Parties and supersedes any prior understanding and agreements among them respecting the subject matter hereof. No amendment
to this Agreement shall be valid unless set forth in writing and signed by both Parties.

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Refers to the entire agreement and its amendment process.

analysis.plain_english_summary: This contract is the final agreement between the parties and any changes must be in writing and signed by both parties.

why: The clause clarifies the scope of the agreement and prevents oral or implied amendments, reducing the risk of misinterpretation or disputes.

comparison: This clause is a standard term in many contracts and is considered best practice.

reasons: The clause is clear and concise, and its meaning is widely understood in the context of contract law.

clause.text:
15.
Notices:
 All notices required or permitted hereunder shall be in writing and shall be sent
by nationally recognized overnight courier service, or by registered or certified mail, to the addresses stated in the heading
of this Agreement. Unless otherwise specified, notices shall be deemed given when the return receipt is received.

classification.label: Return/Destruction
classification.rationale_short: Notice of destruction of information

analysis.plain_english_summary: Notices must be in writing and sent by overnight courier or registered mail to the addresses listed in the agreement.

why: The notice requirements are clear and straightforward, and the method of delivery is reliable.

reasons: The clause is clear and well-defined, but there may be minor variations in delivery methods depending on the specific circumstances.

clause.text:
16.
Non-solicitation/Non-circumvention
. iPass understands, acknowledges and agrees, AS
A MATERIAL INDUCEMENT FOR TEUM TO MAKE AND ENTER INTO THIS AGREEMENT, that with respect to any customer or prospective customer
opportunities that are identified by TEUM (collectively, the 
TEUM Leads
) to iPass, that iPass shall
not negotiate directly or indirectly solicit or otherwise attempt to cause the TEUM Leads enter into any form of agreement with
iPass without the consent of TEUM, which consent may be given or withheld in TEUMs sole discretion. In addition to the foregoing,
and without limitation thereof, iPass shall not circumvent or otherwise engage in any form of direct or indirect communications
with any TEUM Leads without the prior written approval of TEUM, which approval may be given or withheld in TEUMs sole discretion.

 

[REMAINDER
OF PAGE LEFT BLANK. SIGNATURES ON FOLLOWING PAGE.]

 

Pareteum Corporation | 100 Park Ave. | Suite 1600 | New York | NY 10017 | United States
Tel. +1 (212) 984.1096
www.Pareteum.com
NYSE MKT: TEUM

 

 

[SIGNATURE
PAGE TO NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT]

 

 

IN
WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties hereto have caused this Agreement to be executed by their duly authorized representatives as of the
date and year written above
.

 

 

Pareteum Corporation 

 

 

/s/ Denis McCarthy

Name: Denis McCarthy

  

Title: SVP Corporate Developement

  

Date: February 15, 2018

 

iPass: 

 

 

/s/ Darin Vickery 
 

Name: Darin VIckery

 

Title: CFO 

 

Date: December 20, 2017

 

Email: ............................................

 

Pareteum Corporation | 100 Park Ave. | Suite 1600 | New York | NY 10017 | United States
Tel. +1 (212) 984.1096
www.Pareteum.com
NYSE MKT: TEUM
